channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Meta replaces fact-checking with X-style community notes
item.description: Facebook and Instagram owner Meta said Tuesday it's scrapping its third-party fact-checking program and replacing it with Community Notes written by users similar to the model used by Elon Musk's social media platform X. Starting in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sony's Movie Adaptation of Until Dawn Won't Follow Original Storyline - TechRadar
item.description: Sony's live-action adaptation of the videogame Until Dawn won't follow the same story as the game, instead featuring new characters facing new threats.
item.dc:creator: lucy.buglass@futurenet.com (Lucy Buglass), TechRadar

item.title: Keep yourself and your money safe from these four scams
item.description: Authorities are warning people to be wary of pig butchering scams, travel scams, law enforcement scams and utility scams to prevent losing money to fraudsters.
item.dc:creator: Nollyanne Delacruz, The Mercury News

item.title: Santa Clara’s Great America theme park lease could be terminated in three years: new docs
item.description: The lease that enables California's Great America amusement park to operate in Santa Clara might be terminated in about three years.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Google's 2024 Timeline update is like Spotify Wrapped for your travels
item.description: Google has released its Timeline update, which recaps your 2024 journeys and compares your total travel distance to the circumference of the Earth.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Tech leasing boom propels South Bay office market to post-COVID highs
item.description: The Silicon Valley office market has staged a sudden turnaround on the heels of a burst of big deals by tech companies.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: El ‘Buscador 360’ que digitaliza la Justicia madrileña supera el 90% de madurez tecnológica
item.description: Hace unos años atrás la Comunidad de Madrid impulsó el desarrollo del proyecto Buscador 360, una solución capaz de transcribir automáticamente las vistas judiciales para que los magistrados, jueces y letrados puedan encontrar de forma rápida y sencilla aquello que se ha dicho durante una sesión.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: First trailer for Prime Video’s upcoming K-Drama Newtopia teases a dystopia more unhinged than Squid Game's
item.description: Prime Video is already hinting at fresh new TV shows for February 2025, and the first trailer for Newtopia is equal parts exciting, thrilling and silly.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Ecobee's new smart thermostat could save you money on heating – and it's half the price of the latest Google Nest
item.description: The Ecobee Smart Thermostat Essential helps you schedule heating and cooling, and spot opportunities to cut energy consumption.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Samsung Galaxy phones are officially getting Qi2 wireless charging in 2025 – as more Galaxy S25 renders leak
item.description: Android phones are getting their own MagSafe wireless charging update, with the Galaxy S25 first in line.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia unveils new GeForce RTX 5090, RTX 5080, RTX 5070 Ti, and RTX 5070 graphics cards at CES 2025
item.description: Nvidia unveiled its long-awaited GeForce RTX 5090, RTX 5080, and RTX 5070 Ti at its CES 2025 keynote this week.
item.dc:creator: christian.guyton@futurenet.com (Christian Guyton), TechRadar

item.title: Call of Duty: Black Ops 6 and more Xbox Game Pass Ultimate titles will soon be playable on select LG Smart TVs
item.description: Microsoft has announced a new partnership with LG Electronics that will soon bring the Xbox App to select LG Smart TVs.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Your CEO has little idea who IT is or what IT does
item.description: IT may be central to modern existence, but the people and processes of IT remain a mystery to most business executives and colleagues. It’s time to change this. I asked a group of business executives to take out a blank sheet of paper, draw a big circle, and label it “IT People and Processes.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI jobs are among the fastest-growing in the UK
item.description: Many of LinkedIn's fastest-growing jobs in the UK didn’t even exist 25 years ago, and AI features more than once.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: CIO, come crescere di ruolo e fare carriera nel 2025
item.description: Il CIO evolve da manager dell’IT a leader strategico, guidando la collaborazione tra dipartimenti e l’innovazione aziendale. La sinergia con le linee di business e l’adozione di tecnologie come l’intelligenza artificiale diventano essenziali.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: With o3 having reached AGI, OpenAI turns its sights toward superintelligence
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reinvigorated discussion of artificial general intelligence (AGI), boldly claiming that his company’s newest model has reached that milestone.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: CISA says 'no indication' other agencies impacted by Treasury hack
item.description: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) said Monday that there is “no indication” other federal agencies were impacted by the recent breach at the Treasury Department.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: British prime minister condemns 'lies and misinformation' after Musk attacks
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer condemned the “lies and misinformation” undermining the United Kingdom’s democracy and his record after Elon Musk’s continued attacks.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Tesla data helped police after Las Vegas truck explosion, but experts have wider privacy concerns
item.description: Privacy data experts say the deep dive by Elon Musk’s company was impressive, but also shines a spotlight on a difficult question as vehicles become less like cars and more like computers on wheels.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: This 22-year-old made an app to help farmers predict risks to their crops
item.description: Rice feeds half the global population, but for many farmers, growing the grain feels like a high-stakes gamble. One in five sacks is lost to threats including pests, crop disease and extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney's Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2025 ransomware predictions, trends, and how to prepare
item.description: Zscaler ThreatLabz research team has revealed critical insights and predictions on ransomware trends for 2025. The latest Ransomware Report uncovered a surge in sophisticated tactics and extortion attacks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Planes, trains and third-party risks
item.description: Christmas Eve (and Christmas Day) are arguably the most important time-frame for transportation companies. So it was a big deal when an American Airlines system glitch forced the airline to ask the government for a full shutdown on Christmas Eve.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: WordPress.org statement threatens possible shutdown for all of 2025
item.description: Editor’s note: On Jan. 3, 2025, WordPress.org staff announced that they were resuming the suspended services. Automattic CEO Matt Mullenweg on Friday announced a shutdown of almost all services on WordPress.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: L’Oréal’s new cosmetics counter device can predict your skin’s needs
item.description: Choosing skincare products often comes down to a matter of trial and error. A tabletop device unveiled by L’Oréal, called the L’Oréal Cell BioPrint, is intended to change that.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Hacked Chrome extensions put 2.6 million users at risk of data leak
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says hackers are embedding dangerous code in Chrome extensions to steal your data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 great Amazon Prime benefits you’re probably not using
item.description: Let’s address the elephant in the room: Amazon Prime membership costs aren’t getting any cheaper. My credit card just caught the brunt of the yearly auto-renewal.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: DOJ calls on Supreme Court to deny Trump's request to delay TikTok ban
item.description: The Justice Department urged the Supreme Court on Friday to deny President-elect Trump’s request to postpone their ruling on a TikTok ban set to be implemented the day before his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The step I take to clear my inbox every January
item.description: It's easy to find yourself swamped in unread emails. Here are a few simple steps to archiving them and clearing out some of your digital clutter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A federal appeals court ruling deals blow to net neutrality efforts
item.description: A federal appeals court ruled that the FCC lacked authority to restore certain net neutrality rules, handing a blow to Democrats and Biden administration officials who pushed for revived open internet measures.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Working from the office means a pay cut
item.description: In recent months, companies have been forcing workers back into the office with increasing frequency. Annoyingly, many of these are big tech businesses that can most easily enable their workers to work remotely. This trend, likely to continue in 2025, costs workers real money.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The cloud architecture renaissance of 2025
item.description: The perfect storm is coming that will force enterprises to rethink their cloud strategy. Cloud architecture will take center stage during 2025. This isn’t just another hype cycle. First, we need to talk about the elephant in the room: generative AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World
